title: toxicodendron angolense
intro: Toxicodendron angolense, also known as the Angolan poison ivy, is a type of plant that belongs to the Anacardiaceae family, characterized by its irritating sap that causes skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Toxicodendron angolense include its ability to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and reduce blood pressure. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which are often used to treat various skin and health issues, although its use should be approached with caution due to its potential allergenic properties.
